dialogueChunks.intro: You found signs of them, {player_name}! But the trail just got much more complicated.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The markers you recovered confirm that {target_npc} was alive recently. But the trail splits {location_hint}: one path leads toward {enemy_type} territory, the other toward an abandoned area. {quest_item} was found along both routes, as if {target_npc} was deliberately leaving a confusing trail.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're close. I can feel it. But close isn't found, and every wrong turn costs time. The {quest_threat} {quest_threat_is} closing the window we have.

playerResponses.text: Split trails mean someone is being clever. I'll figure out which path is real.
Double the territory to cover, double the danger. But I'll see it through.
Confusing trails in enemy territory? This is too risky for the lead we have.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we found where {target_npc} has been. And the truth is harder than not knowing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} didn't vanish by accident. They were captured by the {enemy_type} and put to work in a labor camp {location_hint}. A refugee who escaped described {target_npc} by name. The camp is guarded, fortified, and deep in hostile territory. We need {quest_item} to sustain a rescue effort.
dialogueChunks.outro: Alive but captive. That's better than the alternatives, but it means the rescue just became ten times harder. Saving the {quest_stakes} depends on moving fast.

playerResponses.text: A labor camp rescue. Tell me everything the refugee told you.
Fortified camp, enemy patrols, time pressure. This is a full operation. I'll need support.
Assaulting a fortified labor camp solo is a death sentence.

dialogueChunks.intro: We found {target_npc}'s shelter, {player_name}. They've been surviving alone this whole time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is alive and hiding in a makeshift camp {location_hint}, but they're injured and can't travel. The area is thick with {enemy_type}. We need {quest_item} to treat their injuries and enough cleared path to get them out. {target_npc} says the {enemy_type} move in waves: there's a narrow window between patrols.
dialogueChunks.outro: They survived alone for weeks in the wilderness. The least we can do is get them the last mile home.

playerResponses.text: Timing and precision. I'll study the patrol patterns and find our window.
An injured person in hostile territory. No time for debates. I'm going.
Timed escapes through monster patrols with an injured person? The math doesn't work.
